'''''UFC 273: Volkanovski vs. The Korean Zombie''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on April 9, 2022, at [[VyStar Veterans Memorial Arena]] in [[Jacksonville, Florida]], United States.<ref name="Jacks">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/02/ufc-273-news-jacksonville-florida-volkanovski-vs-jung-yan-sterling-2|title=UFC 273 heads back to Jacksonville with two title fights atop the bill|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2022-02-04|access-date=2022-02-04}}</ref>

==Background==
This event was initially linked to the [[Barclays Center]] in [[Brooklyn, New York]].<ref name="Brooklyn">{{Cite web|author=Damon Martin|date=2021-12-30|title=UFC 273 targeted for Barclays Center in Brooklyn on April 9|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/12/30/22860694/ufc-273-targeted-for-barclays-center-in-brooklyn-on-april-9|access-date=2021-12-30|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref> However, the promotion opted to move it to [[VyStar Veterans Memorial Arena]] in [[Jacksonville, Florida]].<ref name="Jacks" />

A [[UFC Featherweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Alexander Volkanovski]] and former title challenger [[The Korean Zombie|Chan Sung Jung]] headlined the event.<ref name="titles">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/1/11/22872809/alexander-volkanovski-vs-the-korean-zombie-aljamain-sterling-vs-petr-yan-2-set-to-headline-ufc-273|title=Alexander Volkanovski vs. The Korean Zombie, Aljamain Sterling vs. Petr Yan 2 set to headline UFC 273|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2021-01-11|access-date=2021-01-11}}</ref>

A [[UFC Bantamweight Championship]] title unification rematch between current champion [[Aljamain Sterling]] and former champion/current interim title holder [[Petr Yan]] took place at this event.<ref name="titles" /> The pairing previously met at [[UFC 259]], with Sterling winning the title by disqualification (intentional illegal knee strike) in the fourth round, becoming the first fighter to win a UFC title by disqualification.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/ufc-259-results-highlights-aljamain-sterling-earns-135-pound-title-after-petr-yans-illegal-knee-leads-to-dq/|title=UFC 259 results: Aljamain Sterling earns 135-pound title after Petr Yan's illegal knee leads to DQ|publisher=cbssports.com|author=Dan Doherty|date=2021-01-11|access-date=2022-01-11}}</ref> The rematch was initially expected to take place at [[UFC 267]], however Sterling withdrew from the contest due to lingering neck issues and an interim title bout between Yan and [[Cory Sandhagen]] was set.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/9/25/22693315/aljamain-sterling-out-of-ufc-267-title-fight-vs-petr-yan|title=Aljamain Sterling out of UFC 267 title fight vs. Petr Yan|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck and Damon Martin|date=2021-09-25|access-date=2021-01-11}}</ref> The bout was then scheduled for [[UFC 272]] before being moved to this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/1/5/22869471/alexander-volkanovski-vs-max-holloway-3-aljamain-sterling-vs-petr-yan-2-booked-for-ufc-272|title=Alexander Volkanovski vs. Max Holloway 3, Aljamain Sterling vs. Petr Yan 2 booked for UFC 272|author=Damon Martin|date=2022-01-05|access-date=2022-01-11|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref>

A heavyweight bout between [[Jairzinho Rozenstruik]] and [[Marcin Tybura]] was initially scheduled for [[UFC Fight Night: Makhachev vs. Green]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Tristen Critchfield|date=2022-01-09|title=Heavyweights Jairzinho Rozenstruik, Marcin Tybura to meet at Feb. 26 UFC event|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Heavyweights-Jairzinho-Rozenstruik-Marcin-Tybura-to-Meet-at-Feb-26-UFC-Event-184057|access-date=2022-01-09|publisher=sherdog.com}}</ref> However, in mid January it was announced the bout was moved to this card.<ref name="Rozenstruik vs. Tybura moved">{{Cite web|author=Jesse Holland|date=2022-01-13|title=Jairzinho Rozenstruik vs Marcin Tybura rebooked for UFC 273 on April 9|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2022/1/13/22882164/jairzinho-rozenstruik-vs-marcin-tybura-rebooked-ufc-273-april-9-mma|access-date=2022-01-14|publisher=mmamania.com}}</ref> In turn, the bout was pulled from the card after Tybura withdrew due to an undisclosed illness.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/4/8/23016919/marcin-tybura-falls-ill-ufc-273-fight-jairzinho-rozenstruik-cancelled|title=Marcin Tybura falls ill, UFC 273 fight with Jairzinho Rozenstruik canceled|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2022-04-08|access-date=2022-04-08}}</ref>

[[Wrestling at the 2016 Summer Olympics|2016 Olympic silver medalist in wrestling]] [[Mark Madsen (fighter)|Mark Madsen]] and [[Vinc Pichel]] were expected to meet in a lightweight bout at [[UFC 271]], but they were pushed back to this event due to undisclosed reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2021/11/02/mark-o-madsen-vs-vinc-pichel-set-ufc-271/|title=Mark O. Madsen vs. Vinc Pichel set for UFC 271|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Alex Behunin|date=2021-11-02|access-date=2022-01-19}}</ref><ref name="MadsenPichel">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/01/ufc-271-273-news-mark-madsen-vs-vinc-pichel|title=Mark Madsen vs. Vinc Pichel pushed from UFC 271 to UFC 273|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King and John Morgan|date=2022-01-19|access-date=2022-01-19}}</ref>

A trio of middleweight bouts featuring former interim [[UFC Middleweight Championship]] challenger and [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Jones vs. Team Sonnen]] middleweight winner [[Kelvin Gastelum]] vs. [[Nassourdine Imavov]], [[Dricus du Plessis]] vs. [[Chris Curtis (fighter)|Chris Curtis]], and [[Anthony Hernandez (fighter)|Anthony Hernandez]] vs. Albert Duraev were expected to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/01/ufc-273-kelvin-gastelum-vs-nassourdine-imavov-mma-fight-booking-matchup-announcement|title=Kelvin Gastelum vs. Nassourdine Imavov joins UFC 273 lineup|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2022-01-13|access-date=2022-03-28}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url= https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/1/25/22901708/chris-curtis-vs-dricus-du-plessis-set-for-ufc-273|title=Chris Curtis vs. Dricus du Plessis set for UFC 273|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Shaun Al-Shatti and Mike Heck|date=2022-01-25|access-date=2022-03-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url= https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/02/ufc-273-anthony-hernandez-vs-albert-duraev-booked-april-9|title=Anthony Hernandez vs. Albert Duraev joins UFC 273 lineup on April 9|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farrah Hannoun|date=2022-02-01|access-date=2022-03-15}}</ref> Initially, du Plessis and Hernandez were instead matched up after Curtis and Duraev withdrew due to a wrist and rib injury, respectively.<ref name="Newmatch">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/3/15/22979807/with-chris-curtis-and-albert-duraev-out-dricus-du-plessis-now-faces-anthony-hernandez-at-ufc-273|title= With Chris Curtis and Albert Duraev out, Dricus Du Plessis now faces Anthony Hernandez at UFC 273|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2022-03-15|access-date=2022-03-15}}</ref> However, the bouts were yet again shuffled after Imavov pulled out of the fight vs. Gastelum due to alleged [[visa (document)|visa]] issues which restricted his travel.<ref name="Shakeup">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/03/ufc-273-news-nassourdine-imavov-vs-kelvin-gastelum-off-ilir-latifi-vs-aleksei-oleinik-added|title=UFC 273 news: Nassourdine Imavov vs. Kelvin Gastelum off, Ilir Latifi vs. Aleksei Oleinik added|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2022-03-28|access-date=2022-03-28}}</ref> Gastelum vs. du Plessis and Hernandez vs. Josh Fremd were then set.<ref name="Mixup">{{Cite web|url= https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/3/29/23001651/with-kelvin-gastelum-now-facing-dricus-du-plessis-anthony-hernandez-now-meets-josh-fremd-at-ufc-273|title= With Kelvin Gastelum now facing Dricus Du Plessis, Anthony Hernandez welcomes newcomer Josh Fremd at UFC 273|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Staff|date=2022-03-29|access-date=2022-03-29}}</ref> However, Gastelum withdrew a week before the event due to an undisclosed injury and his bout with du Plessis was cancelled.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/4/1/23007247/kelvin-gastelum-injured-ufc-273-bout-with-dricus-du-plessis-canceled|title=Kelvin Gastelum injured, UFC 273 bout with Dricus du Plessis canceled|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Shaun Al-Shatti|date=2022-04-01|access-date=2022-04-01}}</ref>

A women's bantamweight bout between [[Aspen Ladd]] and [[Irene Aldana]] was expected to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/12/ufc-273-irene-aldana-vs-aspen-ladd-booked|title=Irene Aldana vs. Aspen Ladd targeted for UFC 273|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Danny Segura|date=2022-12-30|access-date=2022-03-27}}</ref> However, Aldana withdrew in late March for undisclosed reasons and was replaced by former [[UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship]] challenger [[Raquel Pennington]].<ref name="Pennington">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/3/26/22997770/with-irene-aldana-out-raquel-pennington-steps-in-to-face-aspen-ladd-at-ufc-273|title=With Irene Aldana out, Raquel Pennington steps in to face Aspen Ladd at UFC 273|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2022-03-26|access-date=2022-03-27}}</ref>

A heavyweight bout between [[Aleksei Oleinik]] and [[Ilir Latifi]] was expected to take place at the event.<ref name="Shakeup" /> They were originally scheduled to meet 2 weeks prior at [[UFC on ESPN: Blaydes vs. Daukaus]] but Latifi withdrew the day of the event due to illness.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/02/ufc-heavyweights-ilir-latifi-aleksei-oleinik-to-square-off-march-26-in-columbus|title=UFC heavyweights Ilir Latifi, Aleksei Oleinik to square off March 26 in Columbus|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King and John Morgan|date=2022-02-04|access-date=2022-03-28}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/3/26/22997714/ilir-latifi-out-of-aleksei-oleinik-fight-at-ufc-columbus|title=Ilir Latifi out of Aleksei Oleinik fight at UFC Columbus|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2022-03-26|access-date=2022-03-28}}</ref> In turn, Latifi withdrew again for undisclosed reasons and was replaced by [[Jared Vanderaa]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/3/31/23004738/aleksei-oleinik-now-fights-jared-vanderaa-at-ufc-273-ilir-latifi-out|title=Aleksei Oleinik now fights Jared Vanderaa at UFC 273, Ilir Latifi out|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2022-03-31|access-date=2022-03-31}}</ref>

At the weigh-ins, [[Kay Hansen]] and [[Julio Arce]] missed weight for their bouts. Hansen weighed in at 118.5 pounds, 2.5 pounds over the strawweight non-title fight limit. Arce weighed in at 136.5 pounds, half a pound over the bantamweight non-title fight limit. Both of their bouts proceeded at a catchweight and they forfeited 20% of their individual purses which went to their opponents Piera Rodríguez and [[Daniel Santos (fighter)|Daniel Santos]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/4/8/23016400/ufc-273-weigh-in-results-alexander-volkanovski-aljamain-sterling-khamzat-chimaev-championship|title=UFC 273 weigh-in results: Both title fights are set, 2 prelim fighters miss|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2022-04-08|access-date=2022-04-08}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref name="Bonuses" >{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/4/10/23018702/ufc-273-post-fight-bonuses-khamzat-chimaev-vs-gilbert-burns-wins-fight-of-the-night|title=UFC 273 post-fight bonuses: Khamzat Chimaev vs. Gilbert Burns wins 'Fight of the Night'|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Newswire|date=2022-04-10|access-date=2022-04-10}}</ref>

* '''Fight of the Night: Khamzat Chimaev vs. Gilbert Burns'''
* '''Performance of the Night: Alexander Volkanovski''' and '''Alexey Oleynik'''

While the UFC usually gives out four bonus awards for each event, three "Fan Bonus of the Night" will be awarded by [[Crypto.com]], as part of their UFC sponsorship incentive fight kits deal, for every pay-per-view event starting with UFC 273.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/4/7/23014643/ufc-to-debut-crypto-com-bitcoin-fan-bonus-of-the-night-for-fighters-at-ufc-273|title=UFC to debut Crypto.com Bitcoin 'Fan Bonus of the Night' for fighters at UFC 273|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2022-04-07|access-date=2022-04-07}}</ref> Viewers could vote up to three times per pay-per-view on Crypto.com/FanBonus, starting from the opening of the PPV preliminary card and ending an hour after the conclusion of the main card. The bonuses are paid in [[bitcoin]] in US dollars ranging from US$30,000 for first place, US$20,000 for second place, and US$10,000 for third place.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/04/ufc-crypto-fan-bonus-of-the-night-bitcoin|title=UFC adds Fan Bonus of the Night for fighters to be paid in bitcoin|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2022-04-07|access-date=2022-04-07}}</ref> The winners of the inaugural awards were:<ref name="Bonuses" />

* '''First Place:''' Khamzat Chimaev
* '''Second Place:''' Alexander Volkanovski
* '''Third Place:''' Petr Yan
